q: A camera is an optical instrument that forms and records an image of an ___.
A diaphragm is placed behind the convex lens to control the amount of light energy falling on the ___.
The aperture is a ___ opening through which the light passes.
The size or the diameter of the aperture is expressed in terms of ___ numbers.
The f-number of a camera is the ratio of the focal length of the lens to the ___ of aperture.
When taking a photograph in dim light a small f-number (wide ___ ) must be used.
Shutter speed is expressed in terms of the ___ time.
The shorter the exposure time, the higher the ___ speed is.
A camera consists of a ___ ring by which the distance between the lens and the film can be adjusted.
The film at the back of the camera is actually a ___ which receives the image.
Chemical reaction occurs when light energy falls on the film and as a result the image of an object is formed on the ___.
The image formed by a camera lens is real and ___.
It is noted that for a distant object its image is formed at ___ of the lens.
The human eye consists of sense organs which are capable of receiving visual images and sending the information to the ___.
The front portion of the eye is covered by a tough, transparent membrane called the ___.
The region between the cornea and the eyelens is filled with a transparent liquid called the ___ humour.
The region between the eyelens and the retina contains a transparent liquid called the ___ humour.
The ___ is the colored portion of the eye that controls the amount of light energy entering the eye.
The pupil is in the centre of the iris and the light passes through ___.
The pupil automatically dilates in dim light and contracts in ___ light.
The ciliary muscles are attached to the ___.
The ciliary muscles can vary the thickness of the eyelens, thus alter the ___ length.
The retina is the light sensitive area of the cells, at the back of the ___.
The ___ spot which is the most light-sensitive spot on the retina is used for viewing very small objects.
Since the yellow spot consists of ___ exclusively, the small objects can be seen clearly.
There is a spot on the retina which has no rods or cones called the ___ spot.
The cornea, the aqueous humour, the eyelens and the vitreous humour are ___ substances.
Farsightedness is a defect of vision in which ___ objects are seen clearly, but objects nearby are blurry.
Nearsightedness is a defect of vision in which near objects are seen clearly, but objects far ___ are blurry.
Farsightedness can be corrected by the use of spectacles with converging lens or ___ lens.
The least distance of distinct vision is about ___ cm.
For normal vision, the maximum distance of distinct vision is ___.
A compound microscope uses lenses to make ___ images of objects that are too small for the unaided eye to see.
A common type of microscope is a ___ microscope.
The lens nearer the object is called the ___.
The lens through which the final image is viewed is called the ___.
The objective produces an image which is real, ___ and larger than the size of the object.
The final image formed is virtual and on the same ___ as the object.
The final image can be seen clearly when the eye is very close to the ___.
The separation of the lenses in a microscope is $L = v_o + ___$.
The magnifying power ($MP$) of a compound microscope is the product of the magnification of the objective and the magnifying power of the ___.
The separation of the lenses in a compound microscope is $L = v_o + ___$.
When the final image is formed at infinity, the condition is $u_e = ___$.
For a large magnifying power of a compound microscope both the objective and the eyepiece should have ___ focal lengths.
Electron microscopes deliver a more detailed image compared to ___ microscopes.
Optical microscopes use ___ or light energy.
Electron microscopes use electrons, which have shorter wavelengths that allows greater ___.
There are two main types of electron microscope: the Scanning Electron Microscope (SEM) and the Transmission Electron Microscope (___).
The image of the specimen in an electron microscope may be viewed by projecting the magnified electron image onto a fluorescent viewing ___.
Electron microscopes are used to investigate the ultrastructure of a wide range of biological and inorganic specimens, metals, and ___.
An optical instrument used for viewing distant objects is called a ___.
The refracting astronomical telescope consists of an objective of ___ focal length.
Galileo invented a ___ telescope.

The magnifying power of the refracting astronomical telescope is defined as the ratio of the angle $\beta$ subtended by the image seen by the eye through the optical instrument to the angle $\alpha$ subtended by the object as seen without ___.
The separation of the lenses in an astronomical telescope is $L = f_o + ___$.
Most telescopes used at the astronomical observatories in the world are ___ telescopes with a concave mirror used as the objective.
The reflecting telescope was first suggested by ___ and this arrangement is called the Newtonian design.
In the terrestrial telescope a concave lens, instead of a convex lens, is used as an eyepiece to produce a final image which is ___.
Binoculars are an optical instrument which consists of a pair of telescopes one for each ___.
Because of the successive total internal reflections in the prisms, the length of the telescope is greatly ___.
The magnifying power of a compound microscope is $MP = \frac{v_o}{u_o} \times \frac{25}{u_e}$. The separation of the lenses is $L = v_o + ___$.
Compound microscopes are used to study living cells and for regular use when magnification is ___.
Optical microscopes use simple lenses, whereas electron microscopes use electrostatic or ___ lenses.
The accelerated electron beam is focused by electrostatic or electromagnetic lenses, and transmitted through the ___.
The image of the specimen may be recorded by a photographic film or by a ___ camera.
The refractive astronomical telescope consists of an objective of long focal length and an eyepiece of ___ focal length.
A real and inverted image of a distant object is formed at the ___ of the objective.
In practice, it is difficult to make a perfect convex lens of large diameter free from defects so that a concave mirror instead of a convex lens is used as an ___.
The parallel rays from a distant object are reflected from the concave mirror and they are allowed to fall on a ___ mirror.
The eyepiece is, in fact, a ___ glass which magnifies the image.
Total reflecting prisms ($90^\circ$-$45^\circ$-$45^\circ$ prisms) are used in binoculars to reduce the ___ of the terrestrial telescope.
Binoculars, therefore, are widely used as ___ telescopes.
The compound microscope consists of two thin ___ lenses.
When the telescope is in normal adjustment the final image of a distant object is formed at ___.
The magnification of an astronomical telescope for infinity is $MP = \frac{f_o}{___}$.
The magnifying power of the refracting astronomical telescope is the ratio of the angle $\beta$ subtended by the image to the angle ___ subtended by the object.
The electron beam carries information about the structure of the specimen that is magnified by the ___ lens system.
In the terrestrial telescope a concave lens is used as an eyepiece to produce a final image which is ___ as shown in Figure.
Optical microscopes use photons or light energy, while electron microscopes use ___.
For a large magnifying power of a compound microscope the focal length of the objective should be shorter than that of the ___.
Optics is the branch of physics that studies the nature and properties of ___.
A laser, acronym for Light Amplification by Stimulated Emission of Radiation, is a source of a narrow beam of intense, monochromatic, ___ light.
The power in a continuous beam can range from a fraction of a milliwatt to around 20 kilowatts in ___ lasers.
In practice, a laser contains three essential elements: laser medium, power supply and ___ ___.
The laser medium is the material emitting the ___ ___.
The power supply delivers energy to the laser medium in the form needed to excite it to ___ light.
Two essential processes to initiate a laser action are (i) ___ ___ and (ii) population inversion.
The output laser beam is narrow, the emission is coherent and the light is ___.
The output of lasers allows the wide use of lasers in ___.
The fibre optics works on the principle of ___ ___ reflection.
Fibre optics refers to the technology that transmits information as light pulses along a glass or ___ fibre.
An optical fibre is composed of two concentric layers, called the ___ and cladding.
The cladding is surrounded by a layer called ___ (plastic buffer) which protects the optical fibre.
The refractive index of core will be slightly ___ than that of cladding.
A useful application of fibre optics is in ___.
In medicine, endoscopy is a non-surgical procedure to examine the interior of a ___ ___ or cavity of the human body.
A spectrometer is a scientific instrument used to separate and measure ___ components of a physical phenomenon.
The optical spectrometer consists of a light source, collimator, ___ element and a telescope.
Three of the most common optical spectrometers are spectrophotometers, spectrofluorometers and ___ ___.
In a spectrophotometer, a ___ element separates light into a spectrum.
The change in the absorption and emission of the light intensity with ___ allows for the identification of the materials.
The laser medium can be a gas, liquid, crystalline solid or ___ crystal.
The ___ ___ is supplied as an electric current, a flash lamp or another laser.
The optical cavity consists of two highly reflective ___.
A partial reflector allows some part of the light to come out as a ___ ___.
Atoms undergoing stimulated emission radiate photons of the same frequency in the same ___.
For stimulated emission to occur, a ___ ___ would be needed.
To sustain a laser action the lower level must be ___ while the upper level is populated.
A typical beam divergence is around a ___.
Lasers are used in the semiconductor industries for ___.
The refractive index of the core is $n_1 = 1.63$ and that of cladding is $n_2 = 1.5$ according to Example ___.
According to Snell's law, the ___ ___ for core to cladding interface is $i_c = 67^{\circ}$.
The optical spectrometer allows the user to rotate the telescope and determine the angle of dispersion for each ___ ___.
Because of refraction and absorption, the light beam ___ and dies off over a certain distance.
Laser range finders are used to determine the ___ to an object.
Lasers are used in shops and business establishments to collect information from the ___ ___ printed on the product.
The collimated beam from a specific light source is dispersed by a dispersion element such as ___ or grating.
A laser beam of wavelength $6328 \mathring{A}$ is emitted as shown in Example ___.
The cladding has a ___ refractive index.
The endoscope uses a bundle of ___ ___ to observe an image inside the body.
Photometry is the science of measuring visible light in units that are weighted according to the sensitivity of ___.
The human eye responds only to light having wavelengths between about 400 nm and ___ nm.
Luminous flux ($\Phi$) is a measurement of the power of visible light produced by a ___.
Luminous intensity ($I_v$) is measured in ___ or lumen per steradian.
The candela is the ___ unit.
A solid angle ($\Omega$) is a measure of the amount of the field of view from some particular point that a given ___ covers.
In SI system, a solid angle is expressed in ___.
The solid angle subtended at the centre by the whole surface area of the sphere is ___ steradian.
Luminance ($L_v$) is the luminous intensity per unit area of light travelling in a ___.
The SI unit for luminance is ___.
Illuminance ($E_v$) is the total luminous flux incident on a surface per ___.
The SI unit for illuminance is ___.
Reading areas in school study halls need potent ___.
The minimum average illuminance should be ___ lux with a high uniformity and low glare.
Farsightedness is a defect of vision in which distant objects are seen clearly, but objects nearby are ___.
Nearsightedness is a defect of vision in which near objects are seen clearly, but objects far away are ___.
Microscope uses lenses to make ___ images of objects that are too small for the unaided eye to see.
The optical instrument used for viewing distant objects is called a ___.
A laser can be considered as a source of a narrow beam of intense, monochromatic, ___ light.
Laser light is used in the visible, infrared or ultraviolet part of the ___.
